Class SoundManager

java.lang.Object
util.SoundManager
public class SoundManager extends Object
A Singleton manager responsible for handling all audio playback in the game. It manages short sound effects (SFX) using AudioClip for low latency, and background music (BGM) using MediaPlayer to support crossfading, playlists, and looping behaviors.

    Method

    Safely halts all audio processing and clears memory caches.

    Resumes the paused background music, smoothly fading its volume back up.

    Sets the global volume multiplier for Background Music and immediately updates the active player.

    Updates the playback looping behavior.

    Sets the global volume multiplier for all Sound Effects.
    void
    stopBgm()
    Fades out and gracefully stops the currently playing background music, releasing its resources.

    Stops a specific sound effect if it is currently playing.

  • Method Details

    • get

      public static SoundManager get()
      Retrieves the global instance of the SoundManager.
      Returns:
      the singleton instance of SoundManager

    • playSfx

      public void playSfx(String name)
      Plays a pre-loaded sound effect using the global SFX volume.
      Parameters:
      name - the unique identifier key of the SFX to play

    • playSfx

      public void playSfx(String name, double volume)
      Plays a pre-loaded sound effect at a specific volume override.
      Parameters:
      name - the unique identifier key of the SFX to play
      volume - the volume to play the SFX at (0.0 to 1.0)

    • stopSfx

      public void stopSfx(String name)
      Stops a specific sound effect if it is currently playing.
      Parameters:
      name - the unique identifier key of the SFX to stop

    • playBgm

      public void playBgm(String path, double fade, LoopMode mode)
      Plays a background music track with a crossfade effect.
      Parameters:
      path - the resource path to the audio file
      fade - the duration in seconds to fade in the music
      mode - the LoopMode dictating how the music loops when finished

    • setPlaylist

      public void setPlaylist(List<String> paths, double fade)
      Sets a playlist of background music tracks and begins playing the first track.
      Parameters:
      paths - a list of resource paths to the audio files
      fade - the duration in seconds to fade in the first track

    • stopBgm

      public void stopBgm()
      Fades out and gracefully stops the currently playing background music, releasing its resources.

    • pauseBgm

      public void pauseBgm()
      Smoothly fades out and pauses the currently playing background music.

    • resumeBgm

      public void resumeBgm()
      Resumes the paused background music, smoothly fading its volume back up.

    • getSfxVolume

      public double getSfxVolume()
      Retrieves the global volume level for sound effects.
      Returns:
      the current global SFX volume (0.0 to 1.0)

    • setSfxVolume

      public void setSfxVolume(double v)
      Sets the global volume multiplier for all Sound Effects.
      Parameters:
      v - the volume level from 0.0 (muted) to 1.0 (max)

    • getBgmVolume

      public double getBgmVolume()
      Retrieves the global volume level for background music.
      Returns:
      the current global BGM volume (0.0 to 1.0)

    • setBgmVolume

      public void setBgmVolume(double v)
      Sets the global volume multiplier for Background Music and immediately updates the active player.
      Parameters:
      v - the volume level from 0.0 (muted) to 1.0 (max)

    • setLoopMode

      public void setLoopMode(LoopMode loopMode)
      Updates the playback looping behavior.
      Parameters:
      loopMode - the new LoopMode to apply to the BGM

    • dispose

      public void dispose()
      Safely halts all audio processing and clears memory caches. Should be called when the application is closing.
